false_positives.message: Ensure Flask forms are protected against CSRF attacks. Use Flask-WTF or implement CSRF tokens manual
Flask running with debug=True in production exposes an interactive debugger and code execution via t

agent_prompt: mustel: Fix these first: [S001] app.py:11 - shell=True passes the command through the shell — command injection risk if any part of the command includes user input. Use a list of arguments instead: subprocess.run(['cmd', 'arg'], shell=False). | [S003] app.py:21 - Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded in source code. Anyone with access to the code can forge session cookies. Generate a strong random key and store it in an environment variable: SECRET_KEY = os.environ['SECRET_KEY']. | [S004] app.py:38 - Possible SQL injection vector through string-based query construction | [S006] app.py:75 - shell=True passes the command through the shell — command injection risk if any part of the command includes user input. Use a list of arguments instead: subprocess.run(['cmd', 'arg'], shell=False). | [S007] app.py:76 - shell=True passes the command through the shell — command injection risk if any part of the command includes user input. Use a list of arguments instead: subprocess.run(['cmd', 'arg'], shell=False). | [S008] app.py:81 - Flask running with debug=True in production exposes an interactive debugger and code execution via the browser. Never deploy with debug=True. Set it via environment: app.run(debug=os.environ.get('FLASK_DEBUG', False)). | [E001] app.py:27 - Module level import not at top of file | [E002] app.py:55 - Line too long (90 > 88) | [E003] app.py:67 - requests call without a timeout will hang indefinitely if the server is unresponsive. Add timeout=30 (or an appropriate value) to every HTTP call. | Other: 2 med-sec (S002,S005), 4 style warnings

mustel: Fix these first: [S001] app.py:25 - Hardcoded AWS credentials found. Use IAM roles, ~/.aws/credentials, or environment variables instead of hardcoding keys in source code. | [S002] app.py:26 - Hardcoded AWS credentials found. Use IAM roles, ~/.aws/credentials, or environment variables instead of hardcoding keys in source code. | [S003] app.py:42 - JWT decoded with signature verification disabled. This means any token — including forged ones — will be accepted. Never disable JWT verification in production. | [S004] app.py:44 - `try`-`except`-`pass` detected, consider logging the exception | [S006] app.py:68 - requests call with a variable URL. If this variable is controlled by user input, it may lead to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF). Ensure the URL is strictly validated or fetched from an allowlist. | [S007] app.py:79 - pickle.load/loads() deserializes arbitrary Python objects. Never unpickle data received from an untrusted or unauthenticated source — it can execute arbitrary code. Use JSON or another safe serialization format instead. | [E001] app.py:18 - `fastapi.Header` imported but unused | [E002] app.py:52 - Line too long (90 > 88) | [E003] app.py:56 - Async functions should not use os.path methods, use trio.Path or anyio.path | [E004] app.py:59 - Async functions should not open files with blocking methods like `open` | [E005] app.py:74 - Raw Request object used in endpoint. Consider using FastAPI's typed query parameters and Pydantic models for automatic validation instead of accessing request data directly. | [E006] app.py:82 - Within an `except` clause, raise exceptions with `raise ... from err` or `raise ... from None` to distinguish them from errors in exception handling | Other: 1 med-sec (S005), 7 style warnings
